title: knautia arvensis subsp. rosea
intro: Knautia arvensis subsp. rosea, also known as the red knawel or red scabious, is a plant species in the borage family (Boraginaceae) characterized by its red or pink flowers. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate blood circulation and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Knautia arvensis subsp. rosea include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including hypertension and cardiovascular diseases.
